name: reporting-sprints
description: Use this skill when you need to report on a sprint
capabilities: Invokes sprint-report-generator agent for automated reporting
Creates structured sprint documentation with standard sections
Captures sprint achievements, blockers, and metrics
Generates reports suitable for stakeholders and teams
author: Emz1998
securitySummary: Static analysis flagged a false positive pattern for cryptographic algorithms. Upon manual review, SKILL.md:3 contains only a YAML frontmatter description field with no cryptographic code. This skill is a simple documentation helper that invokes an agent for sprint reporting. No security risks identified.
valueStatement: Teams struggle to create consistent, comprehensive sprint reports that capture progress and blockers. This skill invokes a specialized agent to generate well-structured sprint documentation automatically.
limitations: Does not connect to external project management tools for live data
Requires existing sprint data as input to generate reports
Cannot access real-time velocity metrics or burndown charts
Output quality depends on the completeness of input information

useCases.title: Weekly Sprint Summary
useCases.description: Generate a concise summary of sprint progress for stakeholder communication. Perfect for managers who need to document completed work, current status, and upcoming priorities.

useCases.title: Retrospective Documentation
useCases.description: Create structured retrospective reports that capture what went well, challenges faced, and action items for improvement. Useful for continuous team improvement.

useCases.title: Stakeholder Status Report
useCases.description: Produce professional status reports for external stakeholders that highlight key deliverables, risks, and timeline adherence without exposing internal team details.

promptTemplates.title: Basic Sprint Report
promptTemplates.prompt: Generate a sprint report for the past two weeks. Include completed items, work in progress, any blockers encountered, and goals for the next sprint.
promptTemplates.scenario: When you need a simple summary of recent sprint activity

promptTemplates.title: Detailed Metrics Report
promptTemplates.prompt: Create a comprehensive sprint report with velocity metrics, burndown status, team capacity utilization, and quality indicators. Format the output for executive review.
promptTemplates.scenario: When stakeholders require data-driven progress documentation

promptTemplates.title: Retrospective Summary
promptTemplates.prompt: Generate a retrospective document covering what went well, what could improve, action items assigned to team members, and process improvement recommendations.
promptTemplates.scenario: After sprint completion for team reflection

promptTemplates.title: Cross-Team Alignment Report
promptTemplates.prompt: Create a report that summarizes dependencies between teams, blocked items requiring external resolution, and coordination needs for the upcoming sprint.
promptTemplates.scenario: For organizations with multiple interdependent teams

bestPractices: Provide specific details about completed work, blockers, and team capacity to improve report quality
Include metrics like story points completed and bugs fixed for quantitative reporting
Review generated reports for accuracy before sharing with stakeholders
Use consistent sprint naming conventions to maintain organized documentation
antiPatterns: Providing vague or incomplete sprint information results in generic reports
Using the skill without reviewing output may spread inaccurate information
Expecting real-time data integration without providing input context
Skipping stakeholder preferences when generating reports for different audiences

faq.question: Does this skill connect to Jira or other project management tools?
faq.answer: No, this skill does not integrate with external tools. You provide the sprint information as input, and it generates a structured report from that data.

faq.question: Can I customize the report format?
faq.answer: Yes, you can specify your preferred format, sections, and detail level in the prompt. The agent will adapt the output to your requirements.

faq.question: What information should I provide for the best results?
faq.answer: Include completed tasks, work in progress, blockers, team capacity, and specific goals. The more context you provide, the more comprehensive the report.

faq.question: Is this skill suitable for formal project documentation?
faq.answer: Yes, it generates professional reports suitable for stakeholder communication. Always review the output before formal distribution.

faq.question: How is this different from asking Claude directly?
faq.answer: This skill invokes a specialized sprint-report-generator agent with specific training on sprint reporting patterns and structure.

faq.question: Can I generate reports for multiple sprints at once?
faq.answer: You can provide information for multiple sprints in a single request, but focused reports for individual sprints typically produce better results.
